---
title: "Manage Project Level Settings"
---

---
title: "Manage Project-level Settings"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/oau1764668776706"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

The project settings enable you to set classification and extraction thresholds and distances, so that the system can accurately classify documents and flag low-confidence cases for review.

You can set a classification review and class assignment threshold and distance as a percentage (e.g., 80%). The parameters can be defined as project-based defaults and also at an class-level.

You can alco view the project limits.

This feature is available to the administrators only.

To manage project level settings:

1. On the project home page, click the ellipsis and then click Settings. The General tabbed area under Settings is displayed.
2. In the General tabbed area, enter a numberical value expressed as a percentage for the following fields. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Class Review Threshold | The minimum confidence value required for a document to get classified without getting flagged for review. |
| Class Assignment Threshold | The minimum confidence value required for a document to get classified. |
| Class Distance | The minimum confidence distance required between classes for successful classification. |
| Classification Page Limit | Maximum number of pages sent for classification. |

3. Click Save. The threshold values are saved.
4. In the Usage tabbed area, view the following details pertaining to the modelling limits and processing limits. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Modelling Limits

- Max. number of fields per class (Max 100)
- Total number of classes (Max 350)
- Total file size (max - 100MB)
- Total number of files ( max - 100)
- Total number of pages (max - 1000)
Processing Limits

- Pages per batch (Max 500)

Note:
The following service limits are applicable when running separation, classification, or extraction during config or runtime.

Separation:

- 500 pages - Max pages in a single request (batch)
- 500 mb - Max individual content file size

Classification

- 500 documents - Max documents in a single request (batch)
- 140 pages - Max pages per document
- 500 mb - Max individual content file size

Extraction

- 500 documents - Max documents in a single request (batch)
-  140 pages - Max pages per document
- 500 mb - Max individual content file size
